fact_verdicts.fact: Governance action id is gov_action1xg69v73lfzkwyhhuz583x6geyc2ewn3r96sxuqj3wqvrrk0yfpksqqa63yc.
fact_verdicts.note: The action id is supplied in the action header.

fact_verdicts.fact: Action type is TreasuryWithdrawals.
fact_verdicts.note: The materials discuss treasury withdrawals and a treasury ask, but the on-chain context block does not state the action type field.

fact_verdicts.fact: Title is "Scalus 2026: Maintenance, Dijkstra Readiness, Interoperability & Application Runtime".
fact_verdicts.note: The action header and anchor body title match exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: Treasury amount is 2464844000000 lovelace, corresponding to ₳2,464,844 in the anchor budget.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor states ₳2,464,844; converted at 1 ADA = 1,000,000 lovelace, this is 2,464,844,000,000 lovelace.

fact_verdicts.fact: Proposed epoch is 640 and the action expires after epoch 647.
fact_verdicts.note: No proposed epoch or expiry epoch is provided in the supplied materials.

fact_verdicts.fact: On-chain explicit DRep ballot counts so far are yes=8, no=4, abstain=2, not stake-weighted.
fact_verdicts.note: The on-chain context states yes=9, no=5, abstain=2, explicit ballots, not stake-weighted.

fact_verdicts.fact: On-chain treasury flow over 36 epochs is inflow 158838297854652 lovelace, enacted withdrawals 361435336000000 lovelace, ratio 2.275492.
fact_verdicts.note: The on-chain context states these exact figures.

fact_verdicts.fact: The proposal asks for ₳2,464,844, described as approximately $394,375 at $0.16/ADA, over 9 months, with no contingency.
fact_verdicts.note: The abstract and budget state these amounts, reference rate, duration, and no contingency.

fact_verdicts.fact: The proposal states the previous version was reduced from ₳8.5M / 12 months to approximately ₳2,464,844 / 9 months, with L1 node and third-party dependencies removed.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor states the previous proposal was ₳8.5M / 12 months and that this version is ₳2,464,844 / 9 months, with L1 node and third-party dependencies removed.

fact_verdicts.fact: The proposal funds maintenance, Dijkstra hard-fork readiness, interoperability, and a scoped application runtime.
fact_verdicts.note: The scope section lists these funded workstreams.

fact_verdicts.fact: The proposal excludes a standalone L1 node, full L2 integration, broad formal verification, and advanced devnet expansion.
fact_verdicts.note: The scope section explicitly excludes these items.

fact_verdicts.fact: The proposal states delivery has three quarterly milestones over July 2026 - March 2027: M1 Q3 2026, M2 Q4 2026, and M3 Q1 2027.
fact_verdicts.note: The milestones section states three quarterly milestones over July 2026 - March 2027 with M1 Q3 2026, M2 Q4 2026, and M3 Q1 2027.

fact_verdicts.fact: Budget distribution is Development & Engineering $315,000 / ₳1,968,750 / 79.9%; Product Management & Delivery $39,375 / ₳246,094 / 10.0%; Documentation & Developer Enablement $25,000 / ₳156,250 / 6.3%; Audits & Assurance $15,000 / ₳93,750 / 3.8%; total $394,375 / ₳2,464,844 / 100%.
fact_verdicts.note: The budget distribution table lists these exact categories and figures.

fact_verdicts.fact: Development & Engineering breakdown is Maintenance 0.5 FTE / $78,750 / ₳492,188; Smart-contract platform & Dijkstra readiness 0.75 FTE / $118,125 / ₳738,280; Interoperability 0.25 FTE / $39,375 / ₳246,094; Application runtime 0.5 FTE / $78,750 / ₳492,188; total 2.0 FTE / $315,000 / ₳1,968,750.
fact_verdicts.note: The Development & Engineering Breakdown table lists these exact workstreams and figures.

fact_verdicts.fact: With Product Management & Delivery at 0.25 FTE, total staffing is 2.25 FTE over 9 months.
fact_verdicts.note: The budget section states this directly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The proposal states funding allocations use a market rate of $210,000 per FTE-year, approximately $100/hour, for senior software engineers and senior product leadership with deep DLT expertise.
fact_verdicts.note: The methodology section states this directly.

fact_verdicts.fact: Administration uses SundaeSwap treasury-contracts framework with treasury.ak and vendor.ak validators, according to the anchor.
fact_verdicts.note: The Administration section names the SundaeSwap treasury-contracts framework and the treasury.ak and vendor.ak validators.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states both SundaeSwap contracts have been independently audited by TxPipe and MLabs and are in production use on mainnet.
fact_verdicts.note: The Smart Contract Escrow section states this directly.

fact_verdicts.fact: Lantr Engineering is named as sole vendor.
fact_verdicts.note: The Administration section has a subsection titled Lantr Engineering as Sole Vendor.

fact_verdicts.fact: An independent oversight board is named with Chris Gianelloni, Matthias Benkort, and Riley Kilgore, with stated organizations/projects.
fact_verdicts.note: The Independent Oversight Board table names those members with organizations and projects.

fact_verdicts.fact: No.Witness Labs is named as independent technical assurer, with quarterly technical reviews to be published.
fact_verdicts.note: The Independent Technical Assurer section states this.

fact_verdicts.fact: The proposal states an external financial auditor will scrutinize Scalus's finances and treasury management, with a goal to publish a report in Q2 2027.
fact_verdicts.note: The Independent Financial Audit section states this directly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The permission scheme states disbursement requires Lantr + board majority; sweep early requires Lantr + any 1 board member; pause milestone requires any 1 board member; resume milestone requires board majority.
fact_verdicts.note: The Permission Scheme table lists these signature requirements.

fact_verdicts.fact: The delegation policy states escrow funds enforce auto-abstain DRep delegation and no SPO delegation.
fact_verdicts.note: The Delegation Policy section states this.

fact_verdicts.fact: The failsafe sweep states funds remaining after expiration sweep back to the Cardano Treasury automatically and cannot be overridden.
fact_verdicts.note: The Failsafe Sweep section states this directly.

fact_verdicts.fact: Prior funding disclosed includes Catalyst F11 ₳200,000 received 100%, Catalyst F11 ₳128,000 received 100%, Catalyst F13 ₳100,000 received 100%, and 2025 Treasury Budget ₳657,692 received 100%.
fact_verdicts.note: The Prior Treasury Funding Disclosure table lists these four entries and received percentages.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states all completed milestones were delivered and publicly reported, and the 2025 Treasury Budget allocation is completed.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor states this after the prior funding table.

fact_verdicts.fact: Reporting commitments include release updates, quarterly reports, a public transaction journal, and Scalus Club sessions every 1.5 months.
fact_verdicts.note: The Reporting section lists these commitments.

fact_verdicts.fact: Measurable adoption indicators include Dijkstra readiness, JVM interoperability, ecosystem reuse, developer reach, application runtime, and documentation/blueprints, with targets and measurement methods listed.
fact_verdicts.note: The Measurable Adoption Indicators table lists those metrics with targets and measurement methods.

fact_verdicts.fact: The risk table identifies Market, Adoption, Technical, and Team risks, all with mitigations.
fact_verdicts.note: The Risks & Mitigations table lists these four risk types and mitigation entries.

fact_verdicts.fact: The doctrine prioritizes treasury stewardship and downside protection above evidence quality and public-benefit ecosystem growth, after constitutional integrity and protocol safety.
fact_verdicts.note: The values hierarchy orders constitutional integrity/protocol safety first, treasury stewardship/downside protection second, evidence quality third, and public-benefit ecosystem growth fourth.

fact_verdicts.fact: The doctrine says if ecosystem growth conflicts with treasury stewardship and evidence is weak, prefer ABSTAIN.
fact_verdicts.note: The conflict handling defaults state this directly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The doctrine says if treasury upside is plausible but non-verifiable, judge directionally on the available public record with a caution penalty; in practice NO or ABSTAIN on thin asks.
fact_verdicts.note: The conflict handling defaults state this directly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The doctrine states the current soft gate scores TreasuryWithdrawals directionally with an explicit -0.10 caution penalty when a complete dossier is missing.
fact_verdicts.note: The Dossier Gate Posture soft gate states this directly.

fact_verdicts.fact: The doctrine says AI has collapsed marginal costs and requested ₳ per deliverable should decrease when work can be substantially accelerated by AI tooling.
fact_verdicts.note: The Foundational Axiom section states AI has collapsed marginal costs and that requested ADA per deliverable should decrease, with the evaluation instruction for AI-accelerated work.

fact_verdicts.fact: The doctrine adopts a rolling-window Net Change Limit framework using available(e) = NCL_annual - sum withdrawals(e - window_size ... e), with window_size = 73 epochs.
fact_verdicts.note: The Rolling Window NCL section gives this equation and defines window_size as 73 epochs.

fact_verdicts.fact: The proposal's constitutionality checklist states it follows a recently established net-change limit of 350M and is within boundaries at submission.
fact_verdicts.note: The constitutionality checklist says the proposal follows a recently established net-change limit of 350M and is within boundaries at submission.

material_discrepancies: Fact 5 invents proposed epoch 640 and expiry after epoch 647; those figures do not appear in the supplied materials.
Fact 6 contradicts the on-chain context: the source says explicit DRep ballot counts are yes=9, no=5, abstain=2, not yes=8, no=4, abstain=2.
contradicted: On-chain explicit DRep ballot counts so far are yes=8, no=4, abstain=2, not stake-weighted.
verification.summary: 35 facts are supported, 1 is contradicted, and 2 are not found. The material issues are the incorrect on-chain ballot counts and the unsupported epoch 640/647 specifics.
